inbound_signal: Observation behavior and six walls replayed, but one stale hash blocked DKR-3; cleanup discovery selected an SDK deactivation contract with ten replayed cases; the generic descendant-cancellation question remains open.
decision_delta: Kept DKR-3 mechanically blocked, sent DKR-1 cleanup to a new validator, and activated DKR-2 without adding any product primitive.
expected_effect: Separate SDK activation deactivation from generic ExecutionContext descendant cancellation, so each layer receives only behavior proven necessary.
evidence_delta: DKR-3 reads 12 of 12 trace dimensions, zero cross-session leaks, zero sensitive exports, six of six held walls, and 11 of 12 resolvable hashes. DKR-1 cleanup candidate reads 10 of 10 cases and preserves 6 of 6 activation behavior while the current product wall remains 1 against 0.
problem_restatement: Close an activation safely, observe it through one declared projection, and determine whether ExecutionContext itself must cancel and join descendants before resources clean up.
alternatives_considered: Add a Lite task or spawn abstraction now
Keep SDK deactivation and generic descendant cancellation as separate evidence questions
assumptions_at_risk: Runtime.deactivate can arbitrate finish without a hidden commit
Existing Lite cleanup timing is sufficient for SDK work owned directly by the session
A generic context signal or cancel operation is enough for nested scalar and stream descendants
dkr_decision: Validate DKR-1 cleanup, continue DKR-2 generic probes, mechanically repair DKR-3 evidence, and keep every CKR and PKR candidate.

hallucination_sweep.claim: Verified DKR-3 behavior makes its malformed checkpoint acceptable.
hallucination_sweep.verdict: rejected; one unresolved evidence hash keeps the exact record blocked

missing_context_sweep.claim: Session deactivation proves generic child-context cancellation semantics.
missing_context_sweep.verdict: rejected; scalar, stream, error, consumer-break, and nested-close probes remain separate

unexpected_results.claim: Observation needs no Lite change, while lifecycle may still expose one generic Lite gap.
unexpected_results.verdict: observation side verified; lifecycle side under DKR-2

anti_goal_violation_review.claim: The current product cleanup wall remains breached despite a candidate replacement contract.
anti_goal_violation_review.verdict: held as a blocking baseline; no product edit dispatched
